conflictOfInterest.details: The accountable reviewer principal is also the candidate's accountable author. Founding-stage policy permits one disclosed principal to operate isolated runs; this review used the distinct academic run ID above and did not inspect the authoring conversation, other review conclusions, or an adjudication draft.

findings.finding: With T = 73, n = 22.4 gives 2.5(22.4) + 18 = 74, not 73. The keyed statement that 22.4 would satisfy a real-valued equation is therefore false for the item's stated model. Because this item explicitly assesses original-equation verification and domain interpretation, the key conflates failure to satisfy the equation with failure to belong to the integer domain and defeats the mapped check-solution objective.
findings.resolution: Either state a separate hypothetical case such as T = 74, for which n = 22.4 satisfies the real-valued equation but is inadmissible as a complete-batch count, or keep T = 73 and key 22.4 as an algebra error that fails substitution before any domain test. Update the prompt, answer, reasoning, acceptable responses, common errors, and affected rubric wording consistently.

findings.finding: The task says the numerical values use mutually compatible units supplied by the task, but it supplies no unit names or symbols. A learner therefore cannot check that Q/r and t0 have the same dimension before adding them, even though unit coherence is a source-mapped boundary of this scene.
findings.resolution: Specify one coherent set of units for Q, r, t, and t0 and show that Q/r has time units, or explicitly declare a coherent symbolic unit set before the numerical substitution.

findings.finding: The sentence defining solving as rewriting a constraint without changing which values satisfy it teaches the same material algebra claim represented by claim-unit-independent-rearrangement, but the orientation scene is absent from that claim's sceneIds and its source note names only the quantity-equation and numerical-equation claims. The central preserve-equality claim is therefore not traceable in the scene where it is first taught.
findings.resolution: Add scene-orientation to claims.json#claim-unit-independent-rearrangement and name that claim in this scene's source note, or otherwise revise the ledger and prose so the scene's material claim coverage is complete.

findings.finding: Direct inspection of NIST Guide to the SI sections 7.1 and 7.11 confirms the lesson's bounded distinction: quantity equations relate quantities and are independent of chosen units, whereas numerical-value equations depend on the units used. The paired quantity-equation and numerical-equation claim statements, scopes, and uncertainty notes do not overstate that source.

Independent recalculation confirms that 5(2x - 3) + 4 = 3x + 31 reduces through reversible steps to 7x = 42 and x = 6; substitution gives 49 on both sides. The worked example's calculation and verification are correct within the declared real-number domain.

limitations: Per the operator's scope restriction, I did not inspect site/. Consequently I did not independently check the public review schema, quorum JSON, graph artifact, or cross-lesson terminology ledger; no matching non-site canonical record for the stated outcome or prerequisite was located.
I did not inspect scripts/, workflows, another lesson pack, the authoring conversation, other review conclusions, or an adjudication draft.
The NIST source was directly accessible and sections 7.1 and 7.11 were checked; no cited source was marked human-only or remained unreachable.

My academic review scope was introductory algebra, domain restrictions, formula rearrangement, and scientific quantity-equation notation. No expertise or evidence limitation prevented a responsible request-changes verdict.
